raw_nodes.title: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
raw_nodes.source_text: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits

key_concepts: Average speed, average velocity, instantaneous velocity
Vector operations: addition, subtraction, multiplication by scalar
Resolution of vectors in a plane
Newton's First, Second, and Third laws
Work done by constant and variable forces

units.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
explicit_chapters: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

chapters.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
chapters.source_chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
chapters.source_heading: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
key_concepts: Centre of mass: two-particle system and rigid body
Hooke's law, Young's modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus
Pressure due to fluid column, Pascal's law
Zeroth law of thermodynamics and temperature definition
First law: heat, work, internal energy

units.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

key_concepts: Equation of state for perfect gas
Assumptions and concept of pressure in kinetic theory

long_chapter_names_reclassified: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

topics: Electric charges and Conservation of charge
subtopics: Coulomb's law, forces between multiple charges, superposition principle, continuous charge distribution
key_concepts: Electric field, electric dipole, torque on a dipole
Gauss's theorem and its applications (wire, plane sheet, spherical shell)

subtopics: Potential difference, due to point charge/dipole/system of charges, equipotential surfaces
key_concepts: Capacitors: capacitance, series/parallel combinations, parallel plate capacitor with dielectric, energy stored

subtopics: Drift velocity, mobility, V-I characteristics (linear/non-linear), resistivity/conductivity, temperature dependence of resistance
key_concepts: Internal resistance, emf vs potential difference
Combination of cells, Kirchhoff's rules, Wheatstone bridge

units.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

subtopics: Oersted's experiment, Biot-Savart law (circular loop), Ampere's law (wire/solenoid)
key_concepts: Force on moving charge and current-carrying conductor
Torque on current loop, magnetic dipole moment, galvanometer conversion

subtopics: Bar magnet as solenoid, magnetic dipole field (qualitative), torque on dipole

units.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

subtopics: Faraday's laws, induced EMF and current, Lenz's Law
Peak/RMS values, reactance/impedance, LCR series circuit (phasors), resonance
key_concepts: Power in AC circuits, power factor
Electromagnetic spectrum and uses of different regions

explicit_chapters: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments

chapters.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
chapters.source_chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
chapters.source_heading: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
subtopics: Reflection, spherical mirrors (formula), refraction, total internal reflection, optical fibers
Refraction at surfaces/lenses (thin lens formula, maker's formula)
Huygen's principle, wave fronts, reflection/refraction of plane waves (proofs)
key_concepts: Interference: Young's double slit experiment (fringe width)

units.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
explicit_chapters: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

chapters.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
chapters.source_chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
chapters.source_heading: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

subtopics: Bohr model: radius, velocity, energy of electron in nth orbit
Composition, size of nucleus, nuclear force
Mass-energy relation, mass defect, binding energy per nucleon variation

chapters.source_chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits

subtopics: Energy bands (qualitative), intrinsic/extrinsic semiconductors, p and n type
